headline: Git Version Control System
description: Git is a distributed version control system created by Linus Torvalds in April 2005 for Linux kernel development. It tracks changes to files, enabling multiple developers to collaborate without a central server. Git is the de facto standard for software version control, used by over 90% of developers (Stack Overflow 2024 Survey) and hosting platforms including GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket. Its distributed model means every clone is a full repository with complete history.

citation.name: Version Control in the Age of AI: Git and Beyond (2025 Survey)
citation.author: multiple
